diagrams.title: Q-Learning Algorithm Flowchart
diagrams.description: Step-by-step execution flow of the Q-learning algorithm showing initialization, action selection, environment interaction, Q-value update, and convergence check
elements: Start/Initialize Q-table with zeros or random values
Action Selection (ε-greedy) - Explore with probability ε, Exploit with probability 1-ε
Environment returns next state s' and reward r
Q-Update: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a)]
Check convergence (Q-values stable or max episodes reached)
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the sequential process of Q-learning and how each step contributes to learning the optimal policy

diagrams.title: Q-Table Structure and Update Mechanism
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the Q-table showing states as rows, actions as columns, and the Q-value update mechanism with the TD error highlighted
elements: TD Error = r + γmax(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a) visual representation
Learning rate α scaling the error
diagrams.purpose: Students visualize how Q-values are stored, accessed, and updated in a tabular representation, reinforcing the mathematical update rule

diagrams.title: Agent-Environment Interaction in Q-Learning
diagrams.description: The fundamental reinforcement learning loop showing how the agent interacts with environment, receives rewards, and updates Q-values
elements: State s sent from environment to agent
Action a sent from agent to environment
Next state s' returned from environment
Q-update block showing temporal difference computation
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the interaction between agent and environment, helping students understand where rewards come from and how states transition

diagrams.title: Q-Learning Update Rule Visualization
diagrams.description: Graphical representation of the Temporal Difference (TD) update showing the relationship between current Q-value, target Q-value, and the error that drives learning
elements: Current Q(s,a) value on number line
Learning rate α (0 < α ≤ 1) controlling step size
Discount factor γ showing how future rewards are weighted
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the intuition behind the update rule - moving current estimate toward the target value by a fraction controlled by learning rate

charts.title: ε-Greedy Action Selection Strategy
charts.content: Decision flowchart for choosing actions: Generate random number r (0-1). If r < ε: explore (choose random action). If r ≥ ε: exploit (choose action with maximum Q-value for current state). This balances exploration of new actions with exploitation of learned knowledge.

charts.title: Q-Learning vs SARSA vs DQN
charts.content: Comparison: Q-Learning (Off-policy, learns optimal policy, can learn from exploratory data, may overestimate Q-values), SARSA (On-policy, learns policy being followed, more conservative, better for stochastic environments), DQN (Deep Q-Network, uses neural network for large state spaces, handles continuous states, requires more training data).

charts.title: Q-Learning Hyperparameters
charts.content: Table with columns: Parameter, Symbol, Typical Values, Effect. Learning rate (α): 0.1-0.5 (high=fast learning but unstable, low=slow but stable). Discount factor (γ): 0.9-0.99 (high=values long-term rewards, low=focuses on immediate rewards). Epsilon (ε): 0.01-0.1 (high=more exploration, low=more exploitation). Number of episodes: 1000-10000 depending on problem complexity.

charts.title: Grid World Example Execution
charts.content: 4x4 Grid World: Agent starts at (0,0), Goal at (3,3) with reward +10. Each step costs -0.1. Obstacles at (1,1), (2,2). Episodes: Initialize Q-table→Select action via ε-greedy→Move to new state→Get reward→Update Q→Repeat until goal reached. Q-values converge showing optimal path through (0,1)→(0,2)→(0,3)→(1,3)→(2,3)→(3,3).

branches: Core Concepts: Markov Decision Process, State-Action Value, Optimal Policy
Update Mechanism: Temporal Difference Learning, Bellman Equation, Q-Update Rule
Algorithm Components: Q-Table, Learning Rate α, Discount Factor γ, Episodes
Applications: Grid World, Game Playing, Robotics, Resource Management
Convergence Conditions: Fixed Policy, Stable Q-Values, Maximum Episodes
Variants: Deep Q-Network (DQN), Double Q-Learning, Dueling DQN
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive overview connecting Q-learning to its mathematical foundations, implementation components, and practical applications - helps students see how all concepts interrelate
